initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine the unique non‑zero color (the “wall”)
wall(W) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, color(V).

% There must be exactly one such colour
:- wall(W1), wall(W2), W1 != W2.

% No other non‑zero colour may appear
:- input(R, C, V), color(V), V != 0, V != W.

% Max row and column indices (0‑based)
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary cells (cells on the outer edge that are not wall)
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = MaxR.
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = 0.
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = MaxC.
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = 0.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are not the wall
outside(R, C) :- boundary(R, C).
outside(R, C) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R = R1 + DR, C = C1 + DC,
    input(R, C, V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% New colour for interior cells (the smallest colour ≠0 and ≠W)
new_colour(K) :- color(K), K != 0, K != W, K = #min {K1 : color(K1), K1 != 0, K1 != W}.

% Output definitions
% 1 – wall cells keep the wall colour
output(R, C, W) :- input(R, C, W).

% 2 – outside non‑wall cells keep their original colour (normally 0)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, outside(R, C).

% 3 – interior cells become the new colour
output(R, C, K) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, not outside(R, C), new_colour(K).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

% Choose the unique answer set
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
</asp>

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation: the only non‑zero colour in the input is the
%  “wall” colour (W).  Cells with colour W stay unchanged.
%  Non‑wall cells that can be reached from the outer boundary
%  through cells that are not W remain 0.
%  All other non‑wall cells (i.e. cells that are surrounded by W)
%  become the smallest colour that does not appear in the input
%  and is not 0.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Input size (may be useful for other constructions)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the (unique) non‑zero colour – the wall colour W
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall(W) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, color(V).

% There must be exactly one such colour
:- wall(W1), wall(W2), W1 != W2.

% No other non‑zero colour may appear
:- input(R, C, V), color(V), V != 0, V != W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary of the grid for cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cells on the outer edge that are not a wall
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = MaxR.   % bottom edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = 0.     % top edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = MaxC. % right edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = 0.    % left edge

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary(R, C).

outside(R, C) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R = R1 + DR, C = C1 + DC,
    input(R, C, V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0, 1).  delta(0, -1).  delta(1, 0).  delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  New colour for interior cells (smallest colour ≠0, ≠W)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
new_colour(K) :-
    color(K), K != 0, K != W,
    K = #min {K1 : color(K1), K1 != 0, K1 != W}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1 – wall cells keep the wall colour
output(R, C, W) :- input(R, C, W).

% 2 – outside non‑wall cells keep their original colour (normally 0)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, outside(R, C).

% 3 – interior cells become the new colour
output(R, C, K) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, not outside(R, C), new_colour(K).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

% The unique answer set: one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation: the only non‑zero colour in the input is the
%  “wall” colour (W).  Cells with colour W stay unchanged.
%  Non‑wall cells that can be reached from the outer boundary
%  through cells that are not W remain 0.
%  All other non‑wall cells (i.e. cells that are surrounded by W)
%  become the smallest colour that does not appear in the input
%  and is not 0.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Input size (may be useful for other constructions)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the (unique) non‑zero colour – the wall colour W
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall(W) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, color(V).

% There must be exactly one such colour
:- wall(W1), wall(W2), W1 != W2.

% No other non‑zero colour may appear
:- input(R, C, V), color(V), V != 0, V != W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary of the grid for cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cells on the outer edge that are not a wall
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = MaxR.   % bottom edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = 0.     % top edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = MaxC. % right edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = 0.    % left edge

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary(R, C).

outside(R, C) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R = R1 + DR, C = C1 + DC,
    input(R, C, V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0, 1).  delta(0, -1).  delta(1, 0).  delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  New colour for interior cells (smallest colour ≠0, ≠W)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
new_colour(K) :-
    color(K), K != 0, K != W,
    K = #min {K1 : color(K1), K1 != 0, K1 != W}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1 – wall cells keep the wall colour
output(R, C, W) :- input(R, C, W).

% 2 – outside non‑wall cells keep their original colour (normally 0)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, outside(R, C).

% 3 – interior cells become the new colour
output(R, C, K) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, not outside(R, C), new_colour(K).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

% The unique answer set: one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

full_program:
% Determine whether there is at least one non‑zero cell (the wall colour)
has_nonzero :- input(_,_,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Guess the wall colour (the unique non‑zero colour)
1 { W : color(W), W != 0 } 1 :- has_nonzero.

% Compute maximal row and column indices
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% A cell is on the outer border if it lies on the edge and is not the wall colour
boundary(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W,
    (R = 0; R = MaxR; C = 0; C = MaxC).

% Flood‑fill from the border through cells that are not the wall colour
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% Cells that are zero and not reachable from the border are the interior cells
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).

% There must be at least one interior zero cell (otherwise the new colour is irrelevant)
interior_zero_exists :- interior_zero(R,C).

% Guess the colour used for all interior zeros (must be different from the wall)
1 { K : color(K), K != 0, K != W } 1 :- interior_zero_exists.

% Ensure K is the smallest colour that does **not** appear in the input (except 0)
% If a colour L < K appears in the input then K would not be minimal – make it unsat
:- interior_zero_exists, L, color(L), L != 0, L < K, input(_,_,L).

% Output rules
% Non‑zero cells (including the wall) keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, not interior_zero(R,C).

% Interior zeros become colour K
output(R,C,K) :- interior_zero(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% Exactly one answer set with one colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation: the only non‑zero colour in the input is the
%  “wall” colour (W).  Cells with colour W stay unchanged.
%  Non‑wall cells that can be reached from the outer boundary
%  through cells that are not W remain 0.
%  All other non‑wall cells (i.e. cells that are surrounded by W)
%  become the smallest colour that does not appear in the input
%  and is not 0.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Input size (may be useful for other constructions)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the (unique) non‑zero colour – the wall colour W
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall(W) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, color(V).

% There must be exactly one such colour
:- wall(W1), wall(W2), W1 != W2.

% No other non‑zero colour may appear
:- input(R, C, V), color(V), V != 0, V != W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary of the grid for cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cells on the outer edge that are not a wall
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = MaxR.   % bottom edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = 0.     % top edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = MaxC. % right edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = 0.    % left edge

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary(R, C).

outside(R, C) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R = R1 + DR, C = C1 + DC,
    input(R, C, V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0, 1).  delta(0, -1).  delta(1, 0).  delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  New colour for interior cells (smallest colour ≠0, ≠W)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
new_colour(K) :-
    color(K), K != 0, K != W,
    K = #min {K1 : color(K1), K1 != 0, K1 != W}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1 – wall cells keep the wall colour
output(R, C, W) :- input(R, C, W).

% 2 – outside non‑wall cells keep their original colour (normally 0)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, outside(R, C).

% 3 – interior cells become the new colour
output(R, C, K) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, not outside(R, C), new_colour(K).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

% The unique answer set: one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
</asp>

We'll write:

% Determine wall colour
wall(W) :- color(W), W != 0.

% Ensure exactly one wall colour
:- #count{W : wall(W)} = 1.

% Determine new colour (smallest colour not 0 or wall)
new_color(K) :-
    color(K), K != 0, K != W,
    K = #min {K1 : color(K1), K1 != 0, K1 != W}.

% Dimensions
total_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)} - 1.
total_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) } - 1.

% Adjacent offsets
adj(0,1). adj(0,-1). adj(1,0). adj(-1,0).

% Boundary source (non‑wall cells on outer edge)
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W, R = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W, R = NR.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W, C = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W, C = NC.

% Reachable from boundary via non‑wall cells
reachable(R,C) :- source(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :-
    reachable(R1,C1), adj(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% Output

% Wall cells stay unchanged
output(R,C,W) :- input(R,C,W).

% Outside non‑wall cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W, reachable(R,C).

% Interior non‑wall cells become new colour
output(R,C,K) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W, not reachable(R,C), new_color(K).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% Ensure each cell has exactly one colour
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Output has the same dimensions as the input.
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% Define output dimensions
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Boundary coordinates (min/max rows/cols)
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% Adjacent offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outside (non-wall reachable)
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 5, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 5, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 5, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 5, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-5 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 5.

% Wall cells (color 5) stay 5
:- input(R, C, 5), output(R, C, Color), Color != 5.

% Outside non-5 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-5 cells become 2
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 2.

```
% Determine output for each cell according to wall (5), outside (reachable) and interior (new colour 2)

% Adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Determine grid dimensions (max indices)
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Source cells: non‑wall cells on the outer edge
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, R = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, R = MaxR.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, C = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, C = MaxC.

% Flood‑fill outward through cells that are not the wall
outside(R,C) :- source(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 5.

% Output definitions
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).                       % wall cells
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), outside(R,C).          % outside zeros
output(R,C,2) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).       % interior zeros become 2

% No other output values are allowed
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,5), Color != 5.
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,0), outside(R,C), Color != 0.
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), Color != 2.

% Each cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
```

% Transformation: wall colour = 5, new colour for interior cells = 2.
% All non‑wall cells that can reach the outer edge through cells
% that are not the wall stay 0; the rest become 2.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Adjacent offsets (4‑connected)

% Maximum row / column indices of the input grid
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, R = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, R = MaxR.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, C = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, C = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill outward through cells that are not the wall
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- source(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 5.

% -------------------------------------------------

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation: the only non‑zero colour in the input is the
%  “wall” colour (W).  Cells with colour W stay unchanged.
%  Non‑wall cells that can be reached from the outer boundary
%  through cells that are not W remain 0.
%  All other non‑wall cells (i.e. cells that are surrounded by W)
%  become the smallest colour that does not appear in the input
%  and is not 0.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Input size (may be useful for other constructions)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the (unique) non‑zero colour – the wall colour W
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall(W) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, color(V).

% There must be exactly one such colour
:- wall(W1), wall(W2), W1 != W2.

% No other non‑zero colour may appear
:- input(R, C, V), color(V), V != 0, V != W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary of the grid for cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cells on the outer edge that are not a wall
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = MaxR.   % bottom edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_row(MaxR), R = 0.     % top edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = MaxC. % right edge
boundary(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, max_col(MaxC), C = 0.    % left edge

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are not the wall
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary(R, C).

outside(R, C) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R = R1 + DR, C = C1 + DC,
    input(R, C, V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0, 1).  delta(0, -1).  delta(1, 0).  delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  New colour for interior cells (smallest colour ≠0, ≠W)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
new_colour(K) :-
    color(K), K != 0, K != W,
    K = #min {K1 : color(K1), K1 != 0, K1 != W}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1 – wall cells keep the wall colour
output(R, C, W) :- input(R, C, W).

% 2 – outside non‑wall cells keep their original colour (normally 0)
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, outside(R, C).

% 3 – interior cells become the new colour
output(R, C, K) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != W, not outside(R, C), new_colour(K).

% Exactly one output per cell
output_cell(R, C) :- output(R, C, _).

% The unique answer set: one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
</asp>

<asp>
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Transformation: wall colour = 5, new colour for interior cells = 2.
% All non‑wall cells that can reach the outer edge through cells
% that are not the wall stay 0; the rest become 2.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Adjacent offsets (4‑connected)
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% Maximum row / column indices of the input grid
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Source cells: non‑wall cells on the outer edge
% -------------------------------------------------
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, R = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, R = MaxR.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, C = 0.
source(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 5, C = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill outward through cells that are not the wall
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- source(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 5.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output specifications
% -------------------------------------------------
% wall cells keep colour 5
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% outside non‑wall cells keep colour 0
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), outside(R,C).

% interior zeros become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Prevent any other colour from appearing at a given cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,5),       Color != 5.
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,0), outside(R,C),  Color != 0.
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), Color != 2.

% Exactly one colour per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

% Count total rows and columns for max
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% There must be at least one non-zero cell (the wall)
1 { W : color(W), W != 0 } 1 :- has_nonzero.
has_nonzero :- input(_,_,V), color(V), V != 0.

% There must be at least one interior zero cell (needs new colour)
has_interior_zero :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).

% Guess the wall colour
1 { W : color(W), W != 0 } 1 :- has_nonzero.

% Guess the interior colour K
1 { K : color(K), K != 0, K != W } 1 :- has_interior_zero.

% K must be the smallest colour not appearing in the input (excluding 0 and W)
% For any colour L < K, if L appears in the input, then K is not minimal -> unsat
:- L, color(L), L != 0, L < K, has_input_colour(L).

% Define predicate for a colour that appears in the input (has_input_colour)
has_input_colour(L) :- input(_,_,L).

% Compute boundary cells (edge cells that are not the wall)
boundary(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != W,
    (R = 0; R = MaxR; C = 0; C = MaxC).

% Flood‑fill from boundary through cells that are not the wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% 4‑connected neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Interior zero cells are those that are zero and not reachable from the boundary
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).

% Output rules
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, not interior_zero(R,C).   % non‑zero cells (including wall) stay unchanged
output(R,C,K) :- interior_zero(R,C).    % interior zeros become colour K

% exactly one colour per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% final choice: exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
